methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $7.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Bear Robotics, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by John Ha that has raised $160M+, was valued against a comparable set of nine same-sector peers at similar and adjacent stages. The peer universe includes eight private companies—including Hadrian ($1.6B Series C), 1X Technologies ($10.0B Series B), Exotec ($2.0B Series D), and Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B)—plus one public comparable. The peer range spans $300M to $10.0B, with a median of $1.6B.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $160M+ total raised as a proxy base value anchored at $7.0B and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implied $11.9B. Peer-median triangulation, based on the nine-company comparable set, directly implied $1.6B. The divergence between these methods reflects the stage-progression method's reliance on a high total-raised proxy versus peer-median's constraint to observed market valuations.

Triangulating both methods produced a range of $1.4B (low) to $13.7B (high), with a median of $6.8B. The wide range reflects material uncertainty in the appropriate stage-progression multiple and the representativeness of the total-raised proxy relative to near-term financing expectations. Key variables requiring clarity include recent investor appetite at Series C+ in robotics and comparable company deal terms.
